gourdSeeds.tip: Defeat the armored general blocking the path. The seed is the reward for the kill — impossible to miss on a normal route.
Clear the chained brute guarding the fog gate, then grapple into the building just behind where he stood. Turn left immediately.
In the building where you first speak with the Tengu, grapple up into the rafters and wall-jump into the hole in the ceiling. The seed is in a chest up there.

After the gate opens, climb the main staircase and grapple onto the high walls beside it to reach the rooftops. Work toward the back-left corner of the main tower for an open window leading to a chest.
Climb the large staircase in the battlefield area to a tower. A merchant on the right side of the tower sells the seed.
Deal with the crow ninjas and guards on the rooftops to reveal a hiding thief. He later relocates to the Dilapidated Temple and sells the seed after enough conversation.

From the upper temple grounds, grapple into the rafters of the large hilltop building and drop through the ceiling hole on the left. Head down the stairs — the seed sits in front of a group of worshipping monks.
Push past the riflemen on the upper path until you reach a climbable wall on the left, near a small hut. The seed is up there.
In the center of the village, at the base of the glowing tree. Guarded by several enemies at once — clear the area first.
From the Palace Grounds checkpoint, head into the building and continue to the last room. The seed is in a chest there — hard to miss.

skills.name: Mikiri Counter
skills.note: Turns unblockable thrust attacks into a guaranteed posture punish. Nearly every fight in the game assumes you have this.

skills.name: Shinobi Eyes
skills.note: Adds a visible cue before dangerous attacks land, on top of the animation tell, so you have more time to react.

skills.name: Whirlwind Slash
skills.note: Hits everything around you at once — great for thinning out grouped trash enemies fast.

skills.name: A Shinobi's Karma: Body
skills.note: Small passive Vitality boost. Cheap and worth grabbing whenever a free skill point is sitting idle.

skills.name: Breath of Life: Light
skills.note: Restores some Vitality on a successful deathblow, making aggressive play self-sustaining.
skills.prerequisite: Requires Whirlwind Slash and A Shinobi's Karma: Body first.

skills.name: Suppressive Deathblow
skills.note: Improves the range and reliability of sneak deathblows on unaware enemies.

skills.name: Ascending Carp
skills.note: Increases posture damage dealt on a clean deflect. Foundational for the deflect-and-punish rhythm the whole game is built around.

skills.name: Descending Carp
skills.note: Increases posture damage on the attack immediately following a deflect. Pairs directly with Ascending Carp.

skills.name: Ichimonji
skills.note: Huge single-hit posture damage on a slow wind-up. Best saved for slow, heavily-armored single targets.

skills.name: Ichimonji: Double
skills.note: A faster two-hit follow-up version of Ichimonji once you're comfortable landing the base move.

skills.name: Mortal Draw
skills.note: Punishes opponents who overextend into fast strings. Long startup, so timing matters more than reflex.

skills.name: Mid-Air Deflection
skills.note: Lets you deflect while airborne instead of only blocking, closing a gap most new players don't expect.

skills.name: High Monk
skills.note: Turns a successful sweep-jump into a follow-up attack window. More useful once sweeps stop feeling threatening.

skills.name: Emma's Medicine: Potency
skills.note: Increases Vitality restored per Healing Gourd use — directly extends your survivability in long fights.

skills.name: Loaded Axe
skills.note: Breaks through guards that shrug off ordinary sword strikes.

skills.name: Loaded Spear
skills.note: Pulls enemies in from range and deals solid posture damage on the hit.

skills.name: Fang and Blade
skills.note: Adds a sword follow-up after a prosthetic hit, extending your combo window.

skills.name: Projected Force
skills.note: Situational area tool — more valuable once you've learned specific enemy patterns than as an early pick.

skills.name: Empowered Ichimonji
skills.note: Improves Ichimonji's speed and posture damage — a straight upgrade once available.

skills.name: Empowered Mortal Draw
skills.note: Significantly upgrades Mortal Draw's damage and tracking against bigger targets.

skills.name: Breath of Life: Shadow
skills.note: A stronger version of the Vitality-on-deathblow effect. Very easy to miss — worth prioritizing as soon as Mushin Arts opens up.

skills.name: Senpou Leaping Kicks
skills.note: A jumping kick option that closes distance safely.
skills.prerequisite: Has multiple skill prerequisites across other trees — naturally a later pickup.
